evals.expected_output: Skill asks 'Are you reviewing a PR or your local working branch?' before doing any diff or analysis. The mode selection question is the first substantive output. (In absence of a user response, the skill may default to local branch and proceed.)
assertions: Output contains a mode selection question referencing 'PR' and 'local' or 'branch'
Output contains at least one finding or a severity table (the review actually ran after mode selection)

evals.prompt: Review my code on my local working branch. For any triage prompts asking 'Fix now or later?', answer 'later' for all findings. Do not generate a report at the end.
evals.expected_output: Skill first runs the PR & Issue Check, finds no open PR for the branch, and prints 'No PR found for this branch — skipping issue and test plan checks' before running the review angles. It then runs all 7 review angles on the branch diff, triages all findings as 'To be fixed', prints a severity counts table and What Looks Good section (no Reviewer To-Do section, since the PR/issue check was skipped). No report file is written to the review/ directory.
assertions: Output contains 'No PR found for this branch' printed before the severity counts table
Output contains a severity counts table (🔴 Important / 🟡 Nit / 🟣 Pre-existing)
Output contains 'To be fixed' (triage status applied)
Output contains 'What Looks Good' section with at least one bullet
Output does NOT contain a 'Reviewer To-Do' section
No file created under review/ directory

evals.prompt: Review my code on my local working branch. For any triage prompts asking 'Fix now or later?', answer 'later' for all findings. Yes, generate a report at the end.
evals.expected_output: Skill first runs the PR & Issue Check, finds no open PR for the branch, and prints 'No PR found for this branch — skipping issue and test plan checks' before running the review angles. It then runs all 7 review angles, triages findings as 'To be fixed', prints the summary, then writes a report file to review/<safe-branch>/report-<timestamp>.md. Report contains severity table, findings with Triage field, and What Looks Good section (no Reviewer To-Do section, since the PR/issue check was skipped).
assertions: Output contains 'No PR found for this branch' printed before the review angles run
A report file is created under review/ (path starts with review/)
Report contains a severity counts table
Report contains 'Triage:' field on at least one finding entry
Report does NOT contain a 'Reviewer To-Do' section

evals.prompt: Review my code on my local working branch. There's no PR open for this branch yet — I just want feedback before I push. For any triage prompts asking 'Fix now or later?', answer 'later' for all findings. Do not generate a report at the end.
evals.expected_output: Skill runs the Analysis Phase's PR & Issue Check first: it checks for an open PR on the current branch via `gh pr view`, finds none, and prints 'No PR found for this branch — skipping issue and test plan checks' — then proceeds straight to the 7 review angles without asking about a linked issue or a test plan. Findings are triaged as 'To be fixed', and the summary has no Reviewer To-Do section since the check that produces those items never ran.
assertions: Output contains 'No PR found for this branch' printed before any review angle findings
Output does NOT contain a reviewer prompt asking for an issue number (the PR & Issue Check is skipped entirely, not run to completion with no issue found)
Output does NOT contain a 'Reviewer To-Do' section
Output still contains a severity counts table and a 'What Looks Good' section (the rest of the review runs normally)
